raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review focus on (A) technical/content critique (logic, claim-evidence alignment, and novelty), (B) language and clarity/copy-editing only, or (C) both A and B?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The section asserts protocol guarantees and empirical outcomes, so verifying the coherence of premises-to-conclusions within the text is crucial.",
    "R1.3": "Because the passage reports performance and availability results, checking that stated claims are supported by the reported evidence is central.",
    "R1.4": "The text claims a 'novel approach,' requiring an assessment of incremental contribution relative to closest prior techniques.",
    "R1.6": "A review typically addresses sentence-level clarity and correctness to reduce ambiguity and improve readability.",
    "R1.7": "The section references prior work and standard mechanisms, making accuracy and sufficiency of citations an important review responsibility.",
    "RX.1": "The review should clearly note what can and cannot be concluded from this single section and any assumptions made.",
    "RX.2": "Feedback should avoid overstating issues or certainty beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "The effort must remain scoped to reviewing this section rather than redesigning the system or speculating beyond the provided content.",
    "RX.4": "Comments should avoid causing undue harm and be framed constructively.",
    "RX.5": "All observations should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided text to maintain auditability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus on (A) technical/content critique (logic, claim-evidence alignment, and novelty), (B) language and clarity/copy-editing only, or (C) both A and B?

rationale.R1.2: The section asserts protocol guarantees and empirical outcomes, so verifying the coherence of premises-to-conclusions within the text is crucial.
rationale.R1.3: Because the passage reports performance and availability results, checking that stated claims are supported by the reported evidence is central.
rationale.R1.4: The text claims a 'novel approach,' requiring an assessment of incremental contribution relative to closest prior techniques.
rationale.R1.6: A review typically addresses sentence-level clarity and correctness to reduce ambiguity and improve readability.
rationale.R1.7: The section references prior work and standard mechanisms, making accuracy and sufficiency of citations an important review responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: The review should clearly note what can and cannot be concluded from this single section and any assumptions made.
rationale.RX.2: Feedback should avoid overstating issues or certainty beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: The effort must remain scoped to reviewing this section rather than redesigning the system or speculating beyond the provided content.
rationale.RX.4: Comments should avoid causing undue harm and be framed constructively.
rationale.RX.5: All observations should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided text to maintain auditability.
